reasoning: Critical correctness failures detected (inverted filter logic + lossy GROUP BY key encoding) must be fixed first. Once corrected, massive performance gap (40× slower than DuckDB) presents aggressive optimization opportunity: thread parallelism on 60+ core system (single-threaded currently), zone maps for I/O reduction, and compact hash tables are proven, high-impact techniques. Iteration 1/3 allows aggressive selection across multiple categories.
focus_areas: Fix inverted l_shipdate filter logic (line 291) — change <= to > for correct semantics
Restore o_orderdate to GROUP BY key encoding (line 306) — use 64-bit encoding with [orderkey:32][orderdate:16][shippriority:16]
Parallelize lineitem scan across 64 cores with thread-local aggregation (morsel-driven)
Implement zone map filtering on l_shipdate to skip non-qualifying blocks (50-70% I/O reduction)
Replace std::unordered_map with compact hash table (absl::flat_hash_map) for 2-3× lookup speedup
strategy_notes: Correctness fixes are non-negotiable prerequisites. After fixes, implement thread parallelism as primary optimization (expected 50-100ms from 3,141ms single-threaded). Zone maps and compact hash tables are complementary: zone maps reduce I/O pressure, hash tables improve CPU efficiency. Join reordering is included as low-risk investigation (current cardinalities suggest modest benefit, but worth validating). Partial aggregation (priority 5) is included to optimize the parallel merge phase. Target: match or beat DuckDB's 77-112ms (SF10) by combining all techniques. The Learner will validate correctness; system rolls back if regression detected.
notes: CRITICAL FIXES FIRST: Both semantic bugs (line 291 and 306) must be corrected before performance work. These are not optimizations — they are correctness requirements. Once fixed, aggressive parallelism is justified: single-threaded execution on 60+ core system wastes 98% of hardware capacity. Estimate post-fix + parallel execution: 50-100ms achievable. Zone maps are already available in storage metadata (idx_l_shipdate_zonemap reference in knowledge base).
